skill_target: groktobench (scoring rubric / scoring script + probe definitions)

rollout_records.analysis: Phase 1 is too easy. Fuzzy matching accepts 'plan' for 'software-development/plan'. No penalty for loading wrong skills first. No decoy detection.

failure_modes: Plan fidelity: passed because content contained the word 'step' — no actual .hermes/plans/ file created
Arxiv fidelity: passed even though model used web_search extensively (still counted as pass)
Excalidraw fidelity: scored based on 'elements' and 'type' keywords in output text, not valid JSON
GitHub fidelity: result_preview check is broken — checks tool output text, not the actual command run
rollout_records.analysis: Fidelity scoring is too generous. Using the wrong tool alongside the right one still counts as 'pass'. Keyword matching over semantic correctness.

failure_modes: Chain accuracy: skill order not checked — ANY appearance in ANY order counts as pass
Context preservation: uses message count as proxy, not actual content continuity
Completion quality: uses output length as proxy, not relevance to prompt
rollout_records.analysis: Chaining scoring is generous. All three sub-scores use structural heuristics (counts, lengths) instead of content quality.

diagnosis: Current scoring is too easy across all phases. A mid-tier model achieves 100% on Phase 1 and generous scores on Phases 2-3. To discriminate between model qualities, all three phases need tighter rubrics, stricter matching, and content-aware evaluation.
